description: Migration up creates online_meetings with PK (tenant, meeting_id) on plain Postgres.
Migration up distributes online_meetings on tenant on Citus (transaction:false, distributeIfNeeded).
Migration down drops online_meetings cleanly on both Postgres and Citus.
Inserting an out-of-enum status value is rejected by the CHECK constraint.
Each of scheduled/ended/recording_pending/recording_ready/no_recording/cancelled/failed is accepted.
Duplicate (tenant, provider, provider_meeting_id) insert violates the unique constraint.
Migration creates online_meeting_artifacts colocated with online_meetings on Citus and rolls back cleanly.
upsertArtifact with the same (tenant, meeting_id, artifact_type, provider_artifact_id) updates rather than duplicates.
Migration inserts an 'Online Meeting' system interaction type with icon 'video'.
Re-running the type migration is idempotent (no duplicate row, no trigger error).
@alga-psa/types exports IOnlineMeeting and IOnlineMeetingArtifact, and IInteraction has optional online_meeting.
OnlineMeetingModel.create + getById round-trips a row including its artifacts array.
getByProviderMeetingId / getByInteractionId / getByAppointmentRequestId return the expected row scoped by tenant.
listPendingRecordings returns only rows in pending-eligible statuses and excludes terminal (no_recording/cancelled/failed).
All model queries are tenant-scoped (a different tenant cannot read another tenant's meeting).
listArtifacts returns a meeting's artifacts newest-first.
InteractionModel.addInteraction(trx) writes inside the passed transaction (rolled back -> no row persisted).
InteractionModel.addInteraction without trx still works via createTenantKnex (back-compat).
Shared helper resolves default status_id when none provided.
Shared helper resolves client_id from contact_name_id when client_id is absent; throws if neither resolves.
Shared helper publishes INTERACTION_LOGGED and INTERACTION_CREATED for meeting-created interactions.
Shared helper triggers revalidatePath for contacts and clients pages.
createTeamsMeeting issues POST /users/{organizerUpn}/events with isOnlineMeeting:true and onlineMeetingProvider teamsForBusiness (mocked fetch).
onlineMeeting id is resolved from the event joinUrl with a URL-encoded JoinWebUrl filter.
Appointment-approval creation sends NO external attendees in the event payload.
MSP-initiated creation includes the provided attendees in the event payload.
updateTeamsMeeting/deleteTeamsMeeting call the events endpoints with the event id (mocked fetch).
Facade result includes organizerUpn, organizerUserId (AAD object id), and eventId, and these persist on online_meetings.
fetchMeetingArtifacts parses recording and transcript collections and fetches transcript content with Accept text/vtt.
fetchMeetingArtifacts URL-encodes organizerUserId, meeting id, and artifact id path segments.
Off-enterprise, the facade fetchMeetingArtifacts binding no-ops (returns nothing / does not call Graph).
approveAppointmentRequest creates an Online Meeting interaction + online_meetings row linking appointment_request_id and interaction_id when creation succeeds.
When capability is unavailable, approve does NOT create an online_meetings row or interaction.
approve still writes appointment_requests.online_meeting_* columns.
Reschedule updates the calendar event via provider_event_id and updates online_meetings + interaction start/end.
Declining/cancelling an appointment sets online_meetings.status='cancelled'.
getOnlineMeetingForInteraction returns the meeting for an interaction scoped to the authenticated tenant, returns null when absent, and rejects missing interaction ids before querying.
A row WITH provider_event_id updates/deletes via the events endpoint.
A legacy row WITHOUT provider_event_id (online_meeting_id only) uses the standalone onlineMeeting handling / warning path.
No Graph create/update/delete call executes inside an open DB transaction (reschedule call is outside withTransaction).
If Graph event creation succeeds but the DB transaction throws, the orphaned calendar event is deleted/cancelled.
An appointment approved before launch has no online_meetings row and is not shown on the timeline; its existing links remain.
scheduleTeamsMeeting happy path creates a meeting for a contact/client.
scheduleTeamsMeeting denies a user lacking the required permission.
scheduleTeamsMeeting defaults the organizer to the tenant service account when none is passed.
scheduleTeamsMeeting fails gracefully (no row) when capability is unavailable.
scheduleTeamsMeeting creates the Online Meeting interaction + online_meetings row via the shared helper.
When a schedule entry is requested, it is created with work_item_type 'interaction' and work_item_id = the created interaction id, linked via schedule_entry_id.
QuickAddInteraction shows the 'Create Teams meeting' toggle only when capability is available and routes save to scheduleTeamsMeeting.
EntryPopup offers 'Generate Teams meeting' when creating a schedule entry and creates a meeting on save.
A schedule entry created with a Teams meeting includes the join URL in its notes/description in both eventMapping copies.
Fetch handler upserts one artifact row per recording and per transcript; re-run does not duplicate rows.
Multiple recordings + multiple transcripts each produce a distinct artifact row.
Transcript content is stored via the internal document helper (NOT uploadDocument) with explicit tenant/user metadata.
Transcript document is associated to client and contact and defaults is_client_visible=false.
Transcript document is created is_client_visible=true only when expose_recordings_in_portal is enabled.
Recording artifact stores content_url; with download_recordings off, no blob is stored (file_id null).
With download_recordings on, the recording blob is stored and the artifact file_id is set.
Status becomes recording_ready when at least one artifact exists.
Status becomes no_recording after recording_fetch_attempts reaches the cap with empty results.
Re-running the handler when a transcript artifact already has a document_id does not create a second document.
refreshMeetingRecordings invokes the shared fetch handler and returns updated artifacts.
Recording proxy streams a playable file and never returns the raw Graph content_url to the client.
Recording proxy rejects unauthenticated or cross-tenant requests.
Recording proxy denies client-portal access when expose_recordings_in_portal is off.
InteractionModel.getById/getForEntity returns online_meeting with its artifacts array populated.
InteractionDetails renders Join, status, Refresh recordings, and lists transcript/recording artifacts with correct actions.
InteractionDetails shows 'Recording pending' and 'No recording' states and the video icon for Online Meeting.
Client portal shows recording/transcript only when expose_recordings_in_portal is on; hidden by default.
New interactive elements have stable kebab-case ids and new copy resolves via i18n keys (no hardcoded strings).
EE migration adds default_meeting_organizer_object_id, download_recordings (default false), expose_recordings_in_portal (default false).
Saving the organizer in the Teams settings UI resolves and persists the AAD object id; the value is read from teams_integrations.
Toggling download_recordings / expose_recordings_in_portal in the Teams settings UI persists to teams_integrations.
Capability check reports recordingsAvailable=false (with a UI warning) when recording/transcript consent is missing.
Capability diagnostics warn when Calendars.ReadWrite lacks Exchange-side mailbox scoping.
CE build: interaction shows the join link only; no recording UI and no runtime errors.
Capture/subscription/job code paths are gated on isEnterprise and inert in CE.
[Phase 2] Subscription creation persists subscription id/expiry on teams_integrations (mocked Graph).
[Phase 2] Renewal job renews a near-expiry subscription and recreates one returning 404 (reuse email webhook renewal test approach).
[Phase 2] Webhook route echoes validationToken, responds fast, and enqueues a job.
[Phase 2] Notification handler resolves the meeting from resourceData/@odata.id (not clientState) and decrypts encrypted resource data.
[Phase 2] clientState is used only for tenant/subscription routing.
